name: IntersectionEvent
summary: Provides all the information for a single Curve Intersection event.

constructors.name: IntersectionEvent
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the IntersectionEvent class

methods.name: CompareEquivalent(IntersectionEvent, IntersectionEvent, Double)
methods.signature: public static bool CompareEquivalent(IntersectionEvent eventA, IntersectionEvent eventB, double relativePointTolerance)

params.name: eventA
params.doc: The first intersection event to compare.

params.name: eventB
params.doc: The second intersection event to compare.

params.name: relativePointTolerance
params.doc: The comparison tolerance. If RhinoMath.UnsetValue, then RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon is used.

returns.doc: true if the two inputs represent the same intersection, false otherwise.

methods.name: CompareEquivalent(IntersectionEvent, IntersectionEvent, Double, TextLog)
methods.signature: public static bool CompareEquivalent(IntersectionEvent eventA, IntersectionEvent eventB, double relativePointTolerance, TextLog log)

params.name: eventA
params.doc: The first intersection event to compare.

params.name: eventB
params.doc: The second intersection event to compare.

params.name: relativePointTolerance
params.doc: The comparison tolerance. If RhinoMath.UnsetValue, then RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon is used.

params.name: log
params.doc: If not null and false is returned, then a description of the error is appended to log.

methods.name: SurfaceOverlapParameter
methods.signature: public void SurfaceOverlapParameter(out Interval uDomain, out Interval vDomain)
methods.summary: If this instance records a Curve|Surface intersection event, and the intersection type if overlap, then use this function to get the U and V domains on the surface where the overlap occurs.

params.name: uDomain
params.doc: Domain along surface U direction for overlap event.

params.name: vDomain
params.doc: Domain along surface V direction for overlap event.

methods.name: SurfacePointParameter
methods.signature: public void SurfacePointParameter(out double u, out double v)
methods.summary: If this instance records a Curve|Surface intersection event, and the intersection type is point, then use this function to get the U and V parameters on the surface where the intersection occurs.

params.name: u
params.doc: Parameter on surface u direction where the intersection occurs.

params.name: v
params.doc: Parameter on surface v direction where the intersection occurs.

properties.name: IsOverlap
properties.summary: All curve intersection events are either a single point or an overlap.

properties.name: IsPoint
properties.summary: All curve intersection events are either a single point or an overlap.

properties.name: OverlapA
properties.summary: Gets the interval on curve A where the overlap occurs. If the intersection type is not overlap, this value is meaningless.

properties.name: OverlapB
properties.summary: Gets the interval on curve B where the overlap occurs. If the intersection type is not overlap, this value is meaningless.

properties.name: ParameterA
properties.summary: Gets the parameter on Curve A where the intersection occurred. If the intersection type is overlap, then this will return the start of the overlap region.

properties.name: ParameterB
properties.summary: Gets the parameter on Curve B where the intersection occurred. If the intersection type is overlap, then this will return the start of the overlap region.

properties.name: PointA
properties.summary: Gets the point on Curve A where the intersection occurred. If the intersection type is overlap, then this will return the start of the overlap region.

properties.name: PointA2
properties.summary: Gets the end point of the overlap on Curve A. If the intersection type is not overlap, this value is meaningless.

properties.name: PointB
properties.summary: Gets the point on Curve B (or Surface B) where the intersection occurred. If the intersection type is overlap, then this will return the start of the overlap region.

properties.name: PointB2
properties.summary: Gets the end point of the overlap on Curve B (or Surface B). If the intersection type is not overlap, this value is meaningless.
